title: Determine the exact value of R(B21, B21)
micro_paper_assessment: Strong structural slice only: any 85-vertex witness must be srg(85,42,20,21), but the exact value remains unresolved until that conference-graph residue is settled.
family_affinity: Strong diagonal one-gap book-Ramsey lane with the endpoint compressed to a conference-graph residue.
generalization_signal: The equality-collapse argument scales to K_{4m+1}: any diagonal B_m witness there must be srg(4m+1,2m,m-1,m), but the final existence/nonexistence step does not scale automatically.
candidate_theorem_slice: If G is a graph on 85 vertices such that neither G nor its complement contains B21, then G is strongly regular with parameters (85,42,20,21).
next_action: Verify whether srg(85,42,20,21) is already known to exist or be impossible; absent that, restrict further solve work to structured conference-graph constructions or sharper conference obstructions.
